lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਪਛਾਣੈ ਮਾਨੈ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਸਮਾਇਦਾ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: guramukh hoe su hukam pachhaanai maanai hukam samaaeidaa |9|
lines.english: One who becomes Gurmukh realizes the Hukam of His command; surrendering to His Command, one merges in the Lord. ||9||
By His Command we come, and by His command we merge into Him again.
By His Command, the world was formed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੇ ਸੁਰਗੁ ਮਛੁ ਪਇਆਲਾ ਹੁਕਮੇ ਕਲਾ ਰਹਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: hukame surag machh peaalaa hukame kalaa rahaaeidaa |10|
lines.english: By His Command, the heavens, this world and the nether regions were created; by His Command, His Power supports them. ||10||
By His Hukam, air, water and fire came into being.
The Hukam of His Command is the mythical bull which supports the burden of the earth on its head.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੇ ਸਿਵ ਸਕਤੀ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸਾ ਹੁਕਮੇ ਖੇਲ ਖੇਲਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: hukame siv sakatee ghar vaasaa hukame khel khelaaeidaa |11|
lines.english: By His Hukam, one dwells in the house of matter and energy - Shiva and Shakti. By His Hukam, He plays His plays. ||11||
By the Hukam of His command, the sky is spread above.
By His Hukam, His creatures dwell in the water, on the land and throughout the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੇ ਸਾਸ ਗਿਰਾਸ ਸਦਾ ਫੁਨਿ ਹੁਕਮੇ ਦੇਖਿ ਦਿਖਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: hukame saas giraas sadaa fun hukame dekh dikhaaeidaa |12|
lines.english: By His Hukam, we draw our breath and receive our food; by His Hukam, He watches over us, and inspires us to see. ||12||
and the uncounted and infinite gods and devils.
By His Hukam, He created His ten incarnations,

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਨੈ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਸੁ ਦਰਗਹ ਪੈਝੈ ਸਾਚਿ ਮਿਲਾਇ ਸਮਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: maanai hukam su daragah paijhai saach milaae samaaeidaa |13|
lines.english: Whoever obeys the Hukam of His Command, is robed with honor in the Court of the Lord; united with the Truth, He merges in the Lord. ||13||
By the Hukam of His Command, the thirty-six ages passed.
By His Hukam, the Siddhas and seekers contemplate Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਨਾਥੁ ਨਥਂ‍ੀ ਸਭ ਜਾ ਕੀ ਬਖਸੇ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਕਰਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: aap naath nathanee sabh jaa kee bakhase mukat karaaeidaa |14|
lines.english: The Lord Himself has brought all under His control. Whoever He forgives, is liberated. ||14||
In the strong fortress of the body with its beautiful doors,

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਥਿਆ ਲੋਭੁ ਨਾਹੀ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸਾ ਲਬਿ ਪਾਪਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: mithiaa lobh naahee ghar vaasaa lab paap pachhutaaeidaa |15|
lines.english: Those gripped by falsehood and greed do not dwell in the celestial home; engrossed in greed and sin, they come to regret and repent. ||15||
Is the king, with his special assistants and ministers.
Chastity, truth and self-control are in the Sanctuary of the Lord.
Truth and contentment govern this body-village.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਹਜਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਜਗਜੀਵਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਪਤਿ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੬॥੪॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: naanak sehaj milai jagajeevan gurasabadee pat paaeidaa |16|4|16|
lines.english: O Nanak, one intuitively meets the Lord, the Life of the World; the Word of the Guru's Shabad brings honor. ||16||4||16||
Maaroo, First Mehl:
He Himself is unattached, infinite and incomparable.
In the Primal Void, the Infinite Lord assumed His Power.
From this Primal Void, He fashioned air and water.
He created the universe, and the king in the fortress of the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਦੇਖੈ ਸੁੰਨਹੁ ਸੁੰਨੁ ਉਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aape kudarat kar kar dekhai sunahu sun upaaeidaa |1|
lines.english: He Himself exercised His Creative Power, and He gazes upon His creation; from the Primal Void, He formed the Void. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਨਿ ਪਾਣੀ ਜੀਉ ਜੋਤਿ ਤੁਮਾਰੀ ਸੁੰਨੇ ਕਲਾ ਰਹਾਇਦਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: agan paanee jeeo jot tumaaree sune kalaa rahaaeidaa |2|
lines.english: Your Light pervades fire, water and souls; Your Power rests in the Primal Void. ||2||
From this Primal Void, Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva issued forth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਪਦ ਵੀਚਾਰੇ ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਪੂਰਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਮਿਲੀਐ ਭਰਮੁ ਚੁਕਾਇਦਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: eis pad veechaare so jan pooraa tis mileeai bharam chukaaeidaa |3|
lines.english: That humble being who contemplates this state is perfect; meeting with him, doubt is dispelled. ||3||
This Primal Void is pervasive throughout all the ages.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਤੁ ਸਤ ਸਰਿ ਮਨੂਆ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਵੈ ਫਿਰਿ ਬਾਹੁੜਿ ਜੋਨਿ ਨ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: tit sat sar manooaa guramukh naavai fir baahurr jon na paaeidaa |4|
lines.english: That human being who becomes Gurmukh, who bathes in the pool of Truth, is not cast into the womb of reincarnation again. ||4||
From this Primal Void, the seven seas were established.
The One who created them, Himself contemplates them.
His Light pervades all the three worlds.
From this Primal Void, came the moon, the sun and the earth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਨੇ ਅਲਖ ਅਪਾਰ ਨਿਰਾਲਮੁ ਸੁੰਨੇ ਤਾੜੀ ਲਾਇਦਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: sune alakh apaar niraalam sune taarree laaeidaa |5|
lines.english: The Lord of this Primal Void is unseen, infinite and immaculate; He is absorbed in the Primal Trance of Deep Meditation. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਥੰਮਾ ਰਾਖੇ ਸਚੁ ਕਲ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: bin thamaa raakhe sach kal paae |
lines.english: He supports them without any visible support, by exercising His True Power.
From this Primal Void, the earth and the Akaashic Ethers were created.
From this Primal Void, came the four sources of creation, and the power of speech.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਸਾਜਿ ਮੇਖੁਲੀ ਮਾਇਆ ਆਪਿ ਉਪਾਇ ਖਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: tribhavan saaj mekhulee maaeaa aap upaae khapaaeidaa |6|
lines.english: He fashioned the three worlds, and the rope of Maya; He Himself creates and destroys. ||6||
They were created from the Void, and they will merge into the Void.

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਤਭੁਜੁ ਚਲਤੁ ਕੀਆ ਸਿਰਿ ਕਰਤੈ ਬਿਸਮਾਦੁ ਸਬਦਿ ਦੇਖਾਇਦਾ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: autabhuj chalat keea sir karatai bisamaad sabad dekhaaeidaa |7|
lines.english: The Supreme Creator created the play of Nature; through the Word of His Shabad, He stages His Wondrous Show. ||7||
From this Primal Void, He made both night and day;
creation and destruction, pleasure and pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖ ਦੁਖ ਹੀ ਤੇ ਅਮਰੁ ਅਤੀਤਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਿਜ ਘਰੁ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: sukh dukh hee te amar ateetaa guramukh nij ghar paaeidaa |8|
lines.english: The Gurmukh is immortal, untouched by pleasure and pain. He obtains the home of his own inner being. ||8||
